reasoning: Real Betis is sitting at 44 points around Matchday 28, while Barcelona and Real Madrid are sitting at 57 points at the same time. There are 10 matches (30 points maximum) left. It is computationally possible for them to catch up (44 + 30 = 74, which would pass 57 if the top teams lost most of their games), so they haven't been mathematically eliminated yet, which means the market shouldn't resolve early. However, the probability of them winning La Liga is effectively zero given the dominance of Barca, Real Madrid, and Atletico Madrid, which is why the Polymarket odds were 0.00 as of March 6. Thus, I assign a 2% chance as a reasonable lower bound to capture "all but mathematically impossible" scenarios and normal tail risk.
